title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-22 · 英国首相辞职与工党领导权更迭
summary: Perdana Menteri Inggris Keir Starmer mengundurkan diri setelah Partai Buruh kalah telak dalam pemilihan lokal, tekanan internal memaksanya turun, dan Wali Kota Manchester Andy Burnham dianggap sebagai kandidat kuat pengganti. Trump mengklaim kolam refleksi Lincoln Memorial dirusak, beberapa orang ditangkap, namun tidak memberikan bukti; kritikus menganggap masalah berasal dari kegagalan proyek perbaikan. Sorotan Piala Dunia: negara kecil seperti Curacao mengejutkan, fusi budaya menjadi fokus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: And it happened right before a fire tore through her home on Friday. She escaped safely with the dog, but as you can see, their house was destroyed in that fire. These are just some of the images, but excellent work, Squilliam, to make sure that you got your owners up and out. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: Dan semua ini terjadi sebelum api melahap rumahnya pada Jumat. Dia selamat bersama anjingnya, tetapi seperti yang Anda lihat, rumah mereka hancur dalam kebakaran itu. Ini hanya sebagian gambar, tetapi kerja bagus, Squilliam, memastikan majikanmu bangun tepat waktu dan keluar. Ini penutup CNN Headline News. Saya Brad Smith. CNN This Morning dengan Otie Cornish dimulai sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Breaking news this morning. A political shakeup in the U.K. Keir Starmer says he is resigning.
paragraphs.cn: Berita utama pagi ini. Gejolak politik Inggris. Keir Starmer mengatakan dia akan mengundurkan diri.

paragraphs.speaker: Keir Starmer
paragraphs.en: That is why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
paragraphs.cn: Itulah mengapa saya akan mengundurkan diri sebagai pemimpin Partai Buruh.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: President Trump blames vandals for his Reflecting Pool problems. How can he be sure?
paragraphs.cn: Presiden Trump menyalahkan pengacau atas masalah kolam refleksi. Bagaimana dia bisa yakin?

paragraphs.speaker: Hunter Biden
paragraphs.en: I'll come campaign for you or against you. Whatever -- whatever helps most.
paragraphs.cn: Saya akan mendukung Anda, atau menentang Anda. Bagaimanapun -- -- yang paling membantu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Gavin Newsom embracing the Bidens as he considers running for president. Is that smart or a counterintuitive strategy? And the World Cup now a must-see spectacle both on and off the field. How did all the critics get it wrong? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with this breaking news. Keir Starmer out. The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour after mounting pressure to step aside.
paragraphs.cn: Gavin Newsom merangkul keluarga Biden saat mempertimbangkan pencalonan presiden. Apakah ini langkah bijak atau strategi kontra-intuitif? Piala Dunia kini menjadi tontonan wajib di dalam dan luar lapangan. Bagaimana semua kritikus salah? Selamat pagi semuanya. Saya Otie Cornish. Kita akan mulai dengan berita utama ini. Keir Starmer keluar. Perdana Menteri Inggris mengumumkan pengunduran dirinya dalam satu jam terakhir setelah menghadapi tekanan yang meningkat untuk turun.

paragraphs.speaker: Starmer
paragraphs.en: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election. I have heard the answer of my Parliamentary party to that question, and I accept that answer with good grace. Every decision I've taken has been about putting the country I love first. That is why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
paragraphs.cn: Pertanyaan yang diajukan partai saya sekarang adalah apakah saya orang yang paling tepat untuk memimpin kita menghadapi pemilihan umum berikutnya. Saya telah mendengar jawaban dari kelompok parlemen atas pertanyaan itu, dan saya menerima jawaban itu. Setiap keputusan yang saya buat adalah untuk menempatkan negara yang saya cintai sebagai prioritas utama. Itulah mengapa saya akan mengundurkan diri sebagai pemimpin Partai Buruh.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We're going to go now to CNN's Clare Sebastian in London. Clare, very busy day there. Can you start by talking about why now? I know it seems inevitable, but what's the sense of why Keir Starmer stepped down?
paragraphs.cn: Kita sekarang terhubung dengan Claire Sebastian dari CNN di London. Claire, hari ini sangat sibuk di sana. Bisakah Anda ceritakan mengapa sekarang? Saya tahu ini tampaknya tak terhindarkan, tetapi apa alasan Keir Starmer turun?

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.en: So, look, the clinching factor for this, Audie, after months of difficulties that the prime minister has faced for a number of different reasons. But the real clinching factor was that we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May, and the Labour Party suffered a massive defeat, losing more than 1,400 local council seats, even as we saw the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge. So, that was a real wake-up call.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, Otie, faktor penentunya adalah Perdana Menteri telah menghadapi kesulitan selama berbulan-bulan karena berbagai alasan. Namun faktor penentu sebenarnya adalah pemilihan lokal di seluruh Inggris pada bulan Mei, di mana Partai Buruh mengalami kekalahan telak, kehilangan lebih dari 1.400 kursi dewan lokal, sementara Partai Reformasi sayap kanan populis yang dipimpin Nigel Farage muncul sebagai kekuatan baru. Jadi, ini benar-benar sebuah peringatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.en: That seemed to suggest to certain parts of the Labour Party that they were going to struggle to defeat Reform in any general election. We have to have a general election in this country by around the middle of 2029. So, that is when the calls for Prime Minister Starmer to go grew even louder.
paragraphs.cn: Ini tampaknya menunjukkan kepada sebagian kalangan di Partai Buruh bahwa mereka akan kesulitan mengalahkan Partai Reformasi dalam pemilihan umum mana pun. Inggris harus mengadakan pemilihan umum sekitar pertengahan 2029. Oleh karena itu, tuntutan agar Perdana Menteri Starmer mundur semakin meningkat.

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.en: But on the flip side, we also have this issue where Andy Burnham, who was then the mayor of Manchester, was seen as the most popular politician within that party, but he was not a member of Parliament, so couldn't officially challenge the prime minister. So, then this process started to try and get him back into Parliament.
paragraphs.cn: Namun di sisi lain, kita juga punya masalah: Wali Kota Manchester Andy Burnham saat itu dianggap sebagai tokoh paling populer di partai, tetapi dia bukan anggota parlemen, sehingga tidak bisa secara resmi menantang Perdana Menteri. Maka, mereka mulai mencari cara untuk mengembalikannya ke parlemen.

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.en: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week, which he won so convincingly that it really strengthened the argument that he might be the man to defeat this right-wing populist party, Reform, in any upcoming election, since he had done it so decisively in this one constituency. So, then, we sort of -- it all coalesced around this point.
paragraphs.cn: Pekan lalu diadakan pemilihan khusus di barat laut Inggris, dan dia menang telak, yang sangat memperkuat argumen bahwa dia mungkin orang yang bisa mengalahkan partai populis sayap kanan 'Partai Reformasi' dalam pemilihan mendatang, karena dia sudah melakukannya dengan tegas di daerah pemilihan ini. Jadi, semua fokus tertuju pada hal ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.en: Keir Starmer spent the weekend thinking about it, addressing the sort of political realities as they stood, and made this extremely difficult decision for him to step down. So, now we have a transition. Take a listen to the prime minister talking about how that will play out.
paragraphs.cn: Keir Starmer menghabiskan akhir pekan untuk merenungkan hal ini, menghadapi realitas politik saat itu, dan membuat keputusan yang sangat sulit -- -- mengundurkan diri. Jadi, sekarang kita berada dalam masa transisi. Dengarkan Perdana Menteri berbicara tentang bagaimana ini akan berlangsung.

paragraphs.speaker: Starmer
paragraphs.en: I will remain in post as prime minister until the contest is complete, and I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power. I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support, knowing that they will inherit a Britain that is far stronger and fairer than the one I inherited two years ago; better prepared for the challenges ahead; and better able to ensure the Labour Party secures a second term in office.
paragraphs.cn: Sampai pemilihan selesai, saya akan terus menjabat sebagai Perdana Menteri dan melakukan segala upaya untuk memastikan transisi kekuasaan yang tertib. Saya juga akan mendukung penuh dan jelas pengganti saya, karena mereka akan mewarisi Inggris yang lebih kuat dan lebih adil daripada saat saya mengambil alih dua tahun lalu; lebih siap menghadapi tantangan masa depan; dan lebih mampu memastikan Partai Buruh memenangkan masa jabatan kedua.

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian
paragraphs.en: So, the question now is, will there be a contest? Will anyone else stand against Andy Burnham? If they do, it could take a bit longer. If they don't, this could all be over in a couple of weeks -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, pertanyaannya sekarang: akankah ada persaingan? Akankah ada yang bersaing dengan Andy Burnham? Jika ada yang maju, prosesnya mungkin lebih lama. Jika tidak, semua ini bisa selesai dalam beberapa minggu -- -- Otie.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. And Burnham, this will be basically third time's the charm, because he has tried to be Labour leader before. Trump ally Nigel Farage actually speaking out after the news of this resignation. Do they feel like this is an opportunity for the Reform Party?
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Bagi Burnham, ini pada dasarnya adalah percobaan ketiga yang berhasil, karena dia sebelumnya dua kali mencalonkan diri sebagai pemimpin Partai Buruh. Sekutu Trump, Nigel Farage, berkomentar setelah berita pengunduran diri. Apakah mereka menganggap ini sebagai peluang bagi Partai Reformasi?

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian
paragraphs.en: Look, they suffered a really big defeat in this special election last week. So, Farage has come out swinging today, calling in a post on X, for a general election and making the point in an essay that Andy Burnham doesn't -- having not been officially elected in a general election -- have a mandate to lead. And pointing back to those local elections at the beginning of May, where Reform did so well. I think look, it is a moment for reform to try to assert themselves.
paragraphs.cn: Dengar, mereka mengalami kekalahan besar dalam pemilihan khusus pekan lalu. Jadi Farage membalas hari ini dengan keras, menyerukan pemilihan umum di postingan X, dan dalam artikelnya menunjukkan bahwa Andy Burnham -- -- karena belum resmi terpilih dalam pemilihan umum -- -- tidak memiliki mandat kepemimpinan. Dia juga menyebut pemilihan lokal awal Mei, di mana Partai Reformasi tampil baik. Saya pikir, ini adalah momen bagi Partai Reformasi untuk menonjolkan eksistensi mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian
paragraphs.en: There are questions about whether they've peaked after Burnham's defeat of the reform candidate in that special election. But as of now, in, you know, opinion polls based on voter intentions for right now, they are leading in the country. So, they are going to be doing everything they can to keep up the momentum. And, of course, the chance now for Labour under a new leader is to try to stabilize their grip on power so they can see off Reform in a general election when it happens -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: Ada yang mempertanyakan apakah Partai Reformasi sudah mencapai puncaknya setelah Burnham mengalahkan kandidat Reformasi dalam pemilihan khusus. Namun untuk saat ini, berdasarkan jajak pendapat preferensi pemilih, mereka unggul secara nasional. Oleh karena itu, mereka akan berusaha sekuat tenaga untuk menjaga momentum. Tentu saja, sekarang kesempatan bagi Partai Buruh di bawah kepemimpinan baru adalah berusaha menstabilkan cengkeraman kekuasaan, sehingga ketika pemilihan umum tiba, mereka bisa mengalahkan Partai Reformasi -- -- Otie.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's Clare Sebastian in London, where U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has stepped down. And coming up on CNN THIS MORNING from: reflection to outrage. Arrests over the algae-plagued Reflecting Pool and the rising costs. Critics are calling it all money down the drain. Plus, are world leaders losing patience with President Trump? Italy's prime minister is the latest. And little countries doing big things at the FIFA World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: Itu tadi Claire Sebastian di London, Perdana Menteri Inggris Keir Starmer telah mengundurkan diri. Selanjutnya di CNN This Morning: dari refleksi hingga kemarahan. Penangkapan dan kenaikan biaya terkait kolam refleksi akibat ganggang. Kritikus mengatakan semua ini sia-sia. Selain itu, apakah para pemimpin dunia kehilangan kesabaran terhadap Presiden Trump? Perdana Menteri Italia adalah contoh terbaru. Juga, negara-negara kecil unjuk gigi di Piala Dunia.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Curacao came to conquer, guys. I'm telling you this once. I'm telling you this twice. Curacao came to show up. Let's go.
paragraphs.cn: Curacao datang untuk menaklukkan, kawan. Saya katakan sekali, saya katakan dua kali. Curacao datang untuk menunjukkan. Ayo berangkat.

paragraphs.speaker: Jeanine Pirro
paragraphs.en: Vandalizing or attempting to vandalize the Reflecting Pool will face the criminal justice system in
paragraphs.cn: Merusak atau mencoba merusak kolam refleksi akan berhadapan dengan sistem peradilan pidana.

paragraphs.speaker: D.C. Cornish
paragraphs.en: Vandalism or something else? President Trump says he knows what is causing the problems at the Reflecting Pool. The president now claiming that multiple people have been arrested, accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. He has not provided evidence to back up that claim. A senior administration official is saying that police arrested five people for vandalism, that they issued citations to five others. Now, Trump did say work will begin immediately to fix it.
paragraphs.cn: Perusakan atau penyebab lain? Presiden Trump mengatakan dia tahu akar masalah kolam refleksi. Presiden kini mengklaim bahwa beberapa orang ditangkap karena diduga merusak kolam refleksi Lincoln Memorial. Dia tidak memberikan bukti untuk mendukung klaim ini. Seorang pejabat senior administrasi mengatakan polisi menangkap lima orang karena perusakan dan mengeluarkan surat panggilan kepada lima orang lainnya. Trump mengatakan perbaikan akan segera dimulai.

paragraphs.speaker: D.C. Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, this comes after his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed: the water riddled with algae, the lining ripped. Visitors say they noticed an increased law enforcement presence following the president's post.
paragraphs.cn: Sebelumnya, proyek perbaikannya senilai $14 juta gagal: air kolam dipenuhi ganggang, lapisan robek. Pengunjung mengatakan mereka melihat peningkatan penegakan hukum setelah postingan presiden.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: I've never seen this many National Guards in the National Park area. They came up to me and two other women, asking if -- if we touched it and then just promoting us not to, that we would get arrested or charged with vandalism if we did.
paragraphs.cn: Saya belum pernah melihat begitu banyak Garda Nasional di area taman nasional. Mereka mendekati saya dan dua wanita lain, bertanya apakah kami menyentuh air kolam, dan memperingatkan kami untuk tidak melakukannya atau akan ditangkap atau didakwa merusak properti.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter with "Semafor"; Julia Manchester, national political reporter for "The Hill"; and Elena Schneider, national political reporter at "NOTUS." Where to begin? Where to begin? Is vandalism the problem, or the green water we just showed you coming out of a hose? I want to play -- the reason why I'm talking about this is Jeanine Pirro, U.S. attorney for D.C., jumped in on this vandalization thing. I don't know if we have that clip.
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini yang bergabung dalam obrolan grup: Eleanor Muller, reporter kebijakan ekonomi Gedung Putih dari "Semafor"; Julia Manchester, reporter politik nasional dari "The Hill"; dan Elena Schneider, reporter politik nasional dari "NOTUS". Mulai dari mana? Apakah vandalisme masalahnya, atau air hijau yang mengalir dari pipa yang baru saja kita lihat? Saya ingin memutar -- -- Saya membicarakan ini karena Jaksa Federal untuk Distrik Columbia, Jean Pierre, juga terlibat dalam kasus vandalisme ini. Saya tidak tahu apakah kita punya video itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But she was asked about this, and I want to play for you what she had to say.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi dia ditanya tentang hal itu, dan saya ingin memutar tanggapannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Pirro
paragraphs.en: There are several citations that have been handed out to individuals. And these are cases that will be prosecuted to the full extent. If there are more serious products that are put into the Reflecting Pool to create more algae or a bigger problem, then we'll consider more serious charges.
paragraphs.cn: Surat panggilan telah dikeluarkan kepada banyak orang. Kasus-kasus ini akan dituntut secara penuh. Jika seseorang memasukkan zat yang lebih serius ke dalam kolam refleksi untuk menciptakan lebih banyak ganggang atau masalah yang lebih besar, kami akan mempertimbangkan tuntutan yang lebih serius.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Help. JULIA MANCHESTER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "THE HILL": You know, it's interesting that this came after we saw a video of hydrogen peroxide being poured into the Reflecting Pool. I'm not a scientist. However. you know, I think it's just interesting to see the sequence of events. Certainly frustrating for the president.
paragraphs.cn: Tolong. Julia Manchester (reporter politik nasional, "The Hill"): Menariknya, ini terjadi setelah kita melihat video seseorang menuangkan hidrogen peroksida ke kolam refleksi. Saya bukan ilmuwan. Tapi saya pikir urutan kejadiannya sangat menarik. Presiden pasti frustrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We should say, the people pouring those jugs of hydrogen peroxide --
paragraphs.cn: Perlu dicatat, orang-orang yang menuangkan kaleng hidrogen peroksida itu -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- were government workers trying to --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- adalah staf pemerintah yang mencoba memperbaiki masalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- repair the work of these contractors that the president invited through no insistence of Congress. Literally, no one asked for this.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Memperbaiki pekerjaan kontraktor yang diundang presiden tanpa desakan Kongres. Sejujurnya, tidak ada yang memintanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And now here we are.
paragraphs.cn: Dan sekarang kita di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: And it's frustrating, I think, for the president, because he's put so much stock, I think, publicly, politically --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir ini membuat presiden frustrasi karena dia sudah banyak berinvestasi, baik secara publik maupun politis -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Frustrating for him.
paragraphs.cn: Ini membuatnya frustrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: And yes, for everyone.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, dan semua orang juga frustrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me play for you one of these tourists. OK? People are walking by, not vandalizing things, though clearly threatened if they are. And here's an example of just someone by the Reflecting Pool, kind of what they see.
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya memutar rekaman seorang turis. Oke? Orang-orang berjalan, tidak merusak apa pun, meskipun jelas terancam jika mereka melakukannya. Ini contoh seseorang di samping kolam refleksi, lihat apa yang mereka lihat.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: So, what do you think?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, bagaimana menurutmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Oh, it's embarrassing. Yes, that's --
paragraphs.cn: Oh, ini memalukan. Ya, itu -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Yes. Money well spent.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Uang terpakai dengan baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: Well, it's a little dirty. So, yes. Just a little.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, agak kotor. Jadi, ya. Hanya agak kotor.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Can you even see the blue?
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah kamu melihat warna biru?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: No, all I see is greens.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, saya hanya melihat hijau.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Out of the mouths of babes. The cost of the Reflecting Pool so far. Originally, we were told it would be 1.5 million. That's not what it is now. Now it is upwards of 14.8 million of green water. ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": And it was also supposed to be done within two weeks. It's obviously not done within two weeks. So, look, I mean, this was a no-bid contract that was given to a pool company that had connections to the Trump administration.
paragraphs.cn: Dari mulut bayi. Biaya kolam refleksi saat ini. Awalnya, kami diberitahu $1,5 juta. Tapi sekarang tidak. Sekarang mencapai $14,8 juta untuk air hijau. Elena Schneider, reporter politik nasional, "NOTUS": Dan seharusnya selesai dalam dua minggu. Jelas tidak selesai dalam dua minggu. Jadi, Anda lihat, ini kontrak tanpa lelang, diberikan kepada perusahaan kolam renang yang memiliki hubungan dengan pemerintahan Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: "The New York Times" has done some great reporting on -- on sort of the questions surrounding who they hired and brought on to do this kind of work. But look, we know what's at the heart of this, which is that Donald Trump cares a lot about the way that Washington, D.C., looks in this moment, particularly as it comes to the 250th anniversary just a couple of weeks away. There is something that he really cares is something that he really cares about, the way that the city looks.
paragraphs.cn: The New York Times melakukan liputan yang bagus tentang keraguan siapa yang mereka pekerjakan untuk pekerjaan semacam ini. Tapi Anda lihat, kita tahu inti masalahnya, yaitu Donald Trump sangat peduli dengan penampilan Washington DC saat ini, terutama hanya beberapa minggu menjelang peringatan 250 tahun. Dia benar-benar peduli dengan tampilan kota ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And one can argue that there are some fountains that are now running in places like Malcolm X Park or outside Union Station, which it is nice to have fountains.
paragraphs.cn: Mungkin ada yang bilang, sekarang beberapa air mancur di Malcolm X Park atau di luar Union Station berfungsi, dan punya air mancur itu bagus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I know, but this is six acres.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tahu, tapi ini enam hektar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And telling people that this public property, which belongs to us, the American people, we can't touch it now, because we are the ones who have vandalized it, I feel like is delusional. ELEANOR MUELLER, WHITE HOUSE ECONOMIC POLICY REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": One of those arrested was a three-time Olympian canoeist who actually gave interviews afterwards, saying that he just bent down. He'd reached into the water. He touched the bottom of the pool.
paragraphs.cn: Memberi tahu orang-orang bahwa properti publik milik rakyat Amerika ini sekarang tidak boleh kita sentuh karena kita yang merusaknya, menurut saya itu konyol. Eleanor Miller, reporter kebijakan ekonomi Gedung Putih, "SEMAFOR": Salah satu yang ditangkap adalah atlet kano Olimpiade tiga kali, yang dalam wawancara setelahnya mengatakan dia hanya membungkuk, memasukkan tangannya ke air, dan menyentuh dasar kolam.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And then when he walked away is when National Park Service federal officers had approached him and said that they were arresting him for vandalism. And so, I'm --
paragraphs.cn: 然后当他走开时，国家公园管理局的联邦官员走近他，说他们以破坏公物罪逮捕他。所以，我 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Like, do you have gold medals? Don't care.
paragraphs.cn: 比如，你有金牌吗？不在乎。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now's the time for your criminal origin story to begin.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是你犯罪起源故事开始的时候了。

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: And I think it says a lot that Jeanine Pirro is the one leading the charge because she's shown herself, again and again, to be the person who's willing to take on Trump's sometimes more political, not to say vendettas, but certainly, agendas against people. She was the -- obviously, same prosecutor who led the investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，珍妮·皮罗带头冲锋这一点很能说明问题，因为她一次又一次地证明了自己是愿意承担特朗普有时更具政治性 -- -- 且不说报复 -- -- 但肯定是针对他人的议程的人。她显然是同一位检察官，曾领导对美联储主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔就 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: -- renovation of the Fed's headquarters.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 美联储总部翻新工程的调查。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we're bringing to bear the power of the state, the legal law enforcement arm --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们正在动用州的力量，即法律执法部门 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- of the district to solve a public relations problem. That's what it looks like on its surface.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 来解决一个公关问题。从表面上看就是这样。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: Yes. I mean, look, I think anyone who lives in Washington, D.C., agrees that, probably, this is not the biggest issue that this city faces right now is, you know, quote, vandalism of people touching the Reflecting Pool. But Jeanine Pirro, like many people in Trump's cabinet, understands that they have an audience of one, and they're going to serve that audience.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，我认为任何住在华盛顿特区的人都会同意，这可能不是这座城市目前面临的最大问题，即所谓的"破坏公物" -- -- 人们触摸倒影池。但珍妮·皮罗，像特朗普内阁中的许多人一样，明白他们只有一个观众，而他们将服务于那个观众。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. You guys, thank you so much. I want you to stay with us as we talk to you. Said more interesting things today. More important things today, like this. Game, set, Serena. Biggest thing in my group chats: the tennis icon will return to singles play at Wimbledon. Plus, the World Cup isn't just about soccer. It's about the vibes, and the cultures coming together are vibing. Good morning to everyone, in the meantime, from the Philadelphia Stadium, where France and Iraq will face off in a game today.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。非常感谢你们。我希望你们继续和我们在一起，我们正在和你们交谈。今天说了更多有趣的事情。今天更重要的事情，比如这个。比赛结束，塞雷娜。我的群聊里最大的消息：这位网球偶像将重返温布尔登单打赛场。另外，世界杯不仅仅是足球。它是关于氛围，文化融合在一起产生的共鸣。与此同时，来自费城体育场的大家早上好，法国和伊拉克今天将在这里进行一场比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Many people probably wrote us off or didn't know much about us, and that was sort of our motivation, as well. Let's show the world that we -- we deserve to compete.
paragraphs.cn: 很多人可能认为我们不行，或者对我们了解不多，这也是我们的动力之一。让我们向世界展示我们 -- -- 我们值得竞争。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Cape Verde and their fairy tale continues. The tiny island nation stunned Spain last week, and they just did it again, ending in a 2-2 draw against Uruguay on Sunday. And they got their first goal in the World Cup, one step closer to a legitimate chance of getting into the knockout stage. So, you've got dreams coming true. That's what the World Cup is all about. And the worries about filling stadiums and selling tickets at this point are gone because of moments like these.
paragraphs.cn: 佛得角，他们的童话还在继续。这个小小的岛国上周震惊了西班牙，而他们刚刚再次做到了，周日以2-2战平乌拉圭。他们打进了世界杯的第一个进球，离真正有机会进入淘汰赛阶段更近了一步。所以，梦想成真了。这就是世界杯的意义所在。而关于填满体育场和售票的担忧，在这样时刻已经烟消云散。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Whether we're watching the next greats score their first World Cup goal or -- of what should be many to come, or seeing a team claim a World Cup victory for the first time in their history. Perhaps one of the most uniting things: seeing different cultures embracing.
paragraphs.cn: 无论我们是在观看下一位伟大球员打进他们的第一个世界杯进球 -- -- 这应该是众多进球中的第一个，还是看到一支球队历史上首次赢得世界杯胜利。也许最团结人心的事情之一：看到不同文化的融合。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: As you pass statues in Boston, you're seeing the Tartan Army puts traffic cones on statues over in Scotland, and they brought that tradition back to Massachusetts.
paragraphs.cn: 当你在波士顿经过雕像时，你会看到苏格兰的"格子呢军团"给雕像戴上交通锥，他们把这一传统带回了马萨诸塞州。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: That's your first ever bite of Texas barbecue. What do you think?
paragraphs.cn: 这是你第一次品尝得克萨斯烤肉。你觉得怎么样？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I don't necessarily know if the United States was the best place for the World Cup, but I do feel like it is a very important place for this to be happening because of the amount of healing that is probably happening to our society.
paragraphs.cn: 我不确定美国是否是举办世界杯的最佳地点，但我确实觉得这是一个非常重要的地方，因为这对我们的社会可能起到很大的疗愈作用。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Joining me now, Will Leitch, contributing editor for "New York Magazine" sports. Nature is healing is what I'm hearing. Why do you think all of this, like, culture clash is such a big deal? And I especially hear it coming out of the South, I should say: the fans from abroad who are visiting there. WILL LEITCH, CONTRIBUTING EDITOR, "NEW YORK MAGAZINE": Yes, to hear the people from the Netherlands talk about their Kansas City barbecue has really been kind of a wonderful aspect of this, too. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。现在请到的是《纽约杂志》体育版特约编辑威尔·莱奇。我听到的是"自然在疗愈"。为什么你认为所有这些文化冲突如此重要？我特别注意到来自南方的声音：那些来自国外的球迷正在那里访问。《纽约杂志》特约编辑威尔·莱奇：是的，听到荷兰人谈论他们的堪萨斯城烤肉，这确实是这件事的一个美妙方面。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You know, remember this was a -- we've talked about this. People were very concerned about coming in -- coming to the United States for a variety of reasons, from immigration to ICE to -- and there have been issues. The Iranian team, in particular, has been treated pretty shabbily by the U.S. government in this. But generally speaking, this tournament has gone off kind of magnificently in a way that has felt pretty healing for the United States at a certain level. You know, it's been a good reminder.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，记得我们之前讨论过。人们出于各种原因非常担心来到美国，从移民到移民和海关执法局 -- -- 而且确实存在问题。特别是伊朗队，在这件事上受到了美国政府相当糟糕的对待。但总的来说，这次赛事进行得非常精彩，在某种程度上对美国起到了疗愈作用。你知道，这是一个很好的提醒。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You know, we're kind of so caught up in the morass of everything that we do have a lot to show people, a lot of things to be proud of. I think you've seen that this is what the World Cup does, right? All of those worries about the World Cup, what's going to happen with this? What's going to happen with that? Once the game gets started, the games themselves are wonderful, but also all of these different cultures. I was fortunate to be at the Spain-Cabo Verde game, that first game in Atlanta.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，我们有点陷入各种事情的泥潭，但我们确实有很多东西可以向人们展示，有很多值得骄傲的事情。我想你已经看到这就是世界杯的作用，对吧？所有关于世界杯的担忧，这个会发生什么？那个会发生什么？一旦比赛开始，比赛本身就很精彩，而且还有所有这些不同的文化。我有幸参加了西班牙对佛得角的比赛，那是亚特兰大的第一场比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And just to -- to celebrate with Cabo Verde fans after. Cabo Verde has fewer people than Mesa, Arizona. I mean, this is a very, very small country. And the joy that they have, that you see this at every single one of these places. These venues have been full. The tickets have been very, very expensive. But the seats are full; the stands are full. So, people are going out to these games and really kind of having a pretty wonderful time.
paragraphs.cn: 之后和佛得角球迷一起庆祝。佛得角的人口比亚利桑那州的梅萨还少。我的意思是，这是一个非常非常小的国家。他们的喜悦，你在每一个地方都能看到。这些场馆都坐满了。门票非常非常贵。但座位都满了；看台都满了。所以，人们都去看这些比赛，并且真的玩得很开心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We talked about Cape Verde, as you mentioned, but we've got to talk about the U.S. Elmo weighed in online. He didn't want to step in it again, as he did with the Knicks. So, he actually, I think, tweeted, "Just to be clear, USA to win. OK? Elmo loves everybody. Just to be clear, don't make this a thing." Honestly, evergreen tweet. But can you talk about how the U.S. is doing?
paragraphs.cn: 我们谈到了佛得角，正如你提到的，但我们还得谈谈美国。艾摩在网上发表了看法。他不想再像对尼克斯队那样踩雷。所以，他实际上，我想，发推文说："说清楚，美国队赢。好吗？艾摩爱大家。说清楚，别搞事情。"老实说，这是永恒的推文。但你能谈谈美国队表现如何吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: We live in an age of Elmo having to say, "Don't make this a thing."
paragraphs.cn: 我们生活在一个艾摩不得不说"别搞事情"的时代。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: As a general rule. But yet, the U.S. team has really -- this is the best team they've ever had in the World Cup. They finished third in 1930. It was a much smaller field and a much different game. The U.S. have looked terrific in a lot of ways, especially Pochettino, their coach, is a big -- He's coached Tottenham Hotspurs to the -- to the Champions League final. He was brought in to kind of fix this team. It has worked. They have played terrific.
paragraphs.cn: 但总的来说，美国队确实 -- -- 这是他们在世界杯上最好的队伍。他们在1930年获得第三名。当时参赛队伍少得多，比赛也大不相同。美国队在很多方面看起来都很棒，尤其是他们的教练波切蒂诺，他很重要 -- -- 他曾带领托特纳姆热刺队进入欧冠决赛。他被请来是为了修复这支队伍。这奏效了。他们踢得非常出色。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: They haven't played any fantastic teams yet, but they've looked organized. They've played great. They've already won their group. We already know when their first game in the knockout round is going to be. It's going to be July 1, and -- it's going to be July 1. And so, it should set up very, very well for them. They have looked exciting. Generally speaking, the U.S., because their talent level has been lower, they've always played kind of a defensive game. They've always kind of quiet, good.
paragraphs.cn: 他们还没有遇到任何非常强的队伍，但他们看起来组织有序。他们踢得很好。他们已经赢得了小组赛。我们已经知道他们淘汰赛的第一场比赛将在7月1日进行。所以，这对他们来说非常有利。他们看起来令人兴奋。总的来说，美国队因为天赋水平较低，一直踢的是防守型比赛。他们一直比较安静，表现不错。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: They've had good keepers and, like, tried to kind of just like fend off attacks. That's not what this team is doing. This team is playing hard. This team is playing fast, and it's scoring a lot of goals. It is very inspiring. You talk about the ticket prices. Initially, they were worries that the U.S. game against Paraguay was not going to sell out.
paragraphs.cn: 他们有过优秀的守门员，也曾试图抵挡进攻。但这支球队不是这样做的。这支球队拼得很凶，打得很快，而且进了很多球。这非常鼓舞人心。说到票价，最初人们担心美国队对阵巴拉圭的比赛会卖不完。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: That's not an issue for any of the rest of these games. There are tickets going for -- the given price for the -- for their first knockout game is now, I think, up to $4,000. They're -- people are very excited about this team in a way that is very new for the U.S. team. Usually, it's just a -- don't embarrass us. Don't -- don't make us look bad on a stage. They're not to the women's level yet, to be very clear.
paragraphs.cn: But the rest of the matches don't have this problem. Ticket prices -- the price for their first knockout match is now, I think, up to $4,000. People are very excited about this team, which is completely new for the US team. Usually, it's just 'don't embarrass us. Don't let us down on the stage.' But clearly, they haven't reached the level of the women's team.

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: The women's are a world power. The U.S. is not there yet, but this is certainly the best men's team they've had in a very, very long time.
paragraphs.cn: The women's team is a world power. The US men's team hasn't reached that level yet, but this is definitely the best men's team they've had in a very long time.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, that's a great reminder. You know, I want to mention this. Iran's team actually left a note in the locker room in L.A. I just want to show people on this little notebook here. It says, "From the ancient Persia of thousands of years ago to the civilized Iran of today, the spirit of Iran remains alive and steadfast. We came to Los Angeles with pride, competed with honor, and leave with dignity. Thank you, Los Angeles, for your hospitality." We weren't sure they'd be able to be here or leave a note like that.
paragraphs.cn: No, that's a good reminder. I want to mention that the Iranian team actually left a note in the locker room in Los Angeles. I want to show everyone this small notebook. It says: 'From ancient Persia thousands of years ago to today's civilized Iran, the spirit of Iran is still alive and strong. We came to Los Angeles with pride, compete with honor, and leave with dignity. Thank you, Los Angeles, for your warm hospitality.' We weren't sure if they could come here or leave such a note.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And it reminds us of what's going on politically. Is there another shoe to drop? Is Trump going to make an appear after FIFA gave him a very special peace adjacent prize?
paragraphs.cn: This reminds us of the political situation. Is there any follow-up? Will Trump show up after FIFA awarded him a very special peace-related award?

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Yes, very special prize. Yes. So, I think that the -- the final is in MetLife Stadium -- or sorry, New York-New Jersey Stadium in -- in East Rutherford. So, it's very possible there could be a Knicks-level-esque appearance for him. Certainly, he's been a big -- he's been a big part. He's been distracted by the pool lately. But generally speaking, he'd been a big part of what was going on with -- with this World Cup. He was at the draw, famously got his -- got his peace prize.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, a very special award. Yes. So, I think the final will be at MetLife Stadium -- or sorry, at the New York-New Jersey stadium in East Rutherford. So, it's very likely he will appear like the Knicks. Of course, he has always been a major figure. Recently he got distracted by the pool. But overall, he has been a significant participant in this World Cup. He attended the draw ceremony and famously received his peace award.

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: And I feel like, for all the worries that like, listen, ICE has not been a major presence in this tournament, like a lot of people worried about. The Iranian team, in particular, has been treated incredibly shabbily in this tournament. They've had to fly out after their games; have not been allowed to stay in the country. They've had to leave, actually, the night of the game to go back to Mexico. Just -- just this weekend U.S.
paragraphs.cn: I think, despite concerns that ICE would be a major presence in this tournament, which didn't happen as many feared, the Iranian team in particular has been treated very poorly. They have to fly out after every match; they are not allowed to stay in the US. Actually, they have to leave on the night of the match and return to Mexico. Just this weekend, the US

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Homeland Secretary Markwayne Mullin claimed that a member of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps was about to get on their plane. The team denies this, says this is in no way true. It -- this -- of all the teams in this, the one team that I think is actually a pretty inspiring story is Iran, in addition to the U.S. team, for the conditions that they've had to play under and have generally succeeded. They've played very well in this tournament.
paragraphs.cn: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin claimed that a member of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps was about to board their plane. The Iranian team denies this, saying it's absolutely not true. Among all the participating teams, I think the Iranian team, besides the US team, is actually a pretty inspiring story, considering the conditions they have to play under and their overall success. They have performed very well in this tournament.

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: There is a non-zero possibility Iran certainly looks like they could advance to the knockout round and keep going. We -- this has been a very -- generally, other than the Iranian team, most of the major fears that people have worried about have not happened. But we still have a long way to go in this tournament. And eventually, the pool may be blue, and there may be something for -- for Trump to -- to turn his attention back to this.
paragraphs.cn: The Iranian team looks very likely to advance to the knockout stage and continue. Apart from the Iranian team, most of the major concerns people had about this tournament have not materialized. But there is still a long way to go in the competition. Eventually, the pool might turn blue, and Trump might turn his attention back here.

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: But so far, it's really been -- the focus has remained with the games and on the international spirit that the U.S. has been able to welcome here, making the Iranian team, even if the government isn't making them feel welcome, the fans are. And I think that speaks to kind of the really wonderful thing about this tournament so far, and really kind of every four years.
paragraphs.cn: But so far, the focus has remained on the matches and the international spirit that the US can welcome here, even if the government didn't make the Iranian team feel welcome, the fans did. I think this reflects the beauty of this tournament and what happens every four years.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, Will Leitch, I know you're in Georgia, and there's like eight games that are going to be there. So, I'm sure you're going to be having a blast. Will Leitch, contributing writer at "New York Magazine." Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: Will Leitch, I know you're in Georgia, where there are probably eight matches. So, I'm sure you'll have a great time. Will Leitch, contributing writer for New York Magazine. Thank you.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to get back to this breaking news. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announcing his resignation. Plus, this here at home: a rash of tornadoes tear through the Midwest. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Now, in CNN This Morning, we'll return to this breaking news. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation. Also, domestic news: a series of tornadoes sweep through the Midwest.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It is now half past the hour. And here's what's happening right now. At least two people are dead after a tornado outbreak in the Mideast. More -- in the Midwest. More than three dozen tornado reports were logged in the region by the storm prediction center. The deaths happened at two separate mobile homes in rural Illinois yesterday. Now that storm system is moving into the mid-Atlantic.
paragraphs.cn: Good morning, everyone. I'm Oti Cornish. Thank you for watching CNN This Morning. It's a little past the half hour. Here's what's happening. A tornado outbreak in the Midwest has killed at least two people. The Storm Prediction Center has recorded more than 36 tornado reports in the region. The deaths occurred yesterday in two mobile homes in rural Illinois. Now, the storm system is moving toward the Mid-Atlantic.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: And the DEA reportedly allowed hundreds of thousands of fentanyl pills to circulate in New Mexico between 2023 and 2025. That's according to three current and former DEA agents who spoke with the "Associated Press." It was a controversial tactic aimed at building larger criminal cases against drug traffickers. They claim, quote, "we 100 percent got people killed. And this morning, Vice President J.D. Vance is in Switzerland for high-level diplomatic talks with Iranian officials.
paragraphs.cn: The DEA reportedly allowed hundreds of thousands of fentanyl pills to circulate in New Mexico between 2023 and 2025. This is according to three current and former DEA agents who spoke to the Associated Press. It's a controversial strategy aimed at building larger criminal cases against drug traffickers. They claim, 'We are 100% responsible for deaths.' This morning, Vice President JD Vance is in Switzerland for high-level diplomatic talks with Iranian officials.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Sunday the two sides held marathon talks to try to establish a long term peace agreement with Iran. But the talks so far have been described as positive and constructive according to mediators from Pakistan and Qatar. And this morning, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, resigning.
paragraphs.cn: On Sunday, both sides held marathon talks trying to reach a long-term peace agreement with Iran. But according to mediators from Pakistan and Qatar, the talks have been described as positive and constructive so far. This morning, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned.

paragraphs.speaker: Keir Starmer
paragraphs.en: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election. I have heard the answer of my parliamentary party to that question. And I accept that answer with good grace.
paragraphs.cn: The question my party is asking now is whether I am the best person to lead us into the next general election. I have heard my parliamentary party's answer to that question. I accept that answer.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, nominations to select a new Labour Party leader are set to open early next month. Starmer says he hopes a new prime minister will be in place when parliament returns from summer recess. That's in September. Starmer's resignation comes just shy of two years into his tenure. Joining me now to discuss, Patrick Baker, host of "Politico's" "Westminster Insider" podcast. Good morning. Thank you so much for being here. PATRICK BAKER, HOST, "POLITICO'S" "WESTMINSTER INSIDER"
paragraphs.cn: Now, nominations for the election of a new Labour leader will begin early next month. Starmer said he hopes a new prime minister will be in place by September, after the parliamentary summer recess. Starmer resigned just shy of two years into his term. Joining me now is Patrick Baker, host of the 'Westminster Insider' podcast from Politico. Good morning. Thank you so much for being here.

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.en: Hi. Great to be with you.
paragraphs.cn: Hello. Great to be with you.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, I just want to remind people, there's been almost half a dozen prime ministers since Brexit, and it's been a decade since Brexit. So, I think, to the American public, we see a lot of churn. What was it that took Starmer down in the end?
paragraphs.cn: Now, I want to remind everyone that since Brexit, there have been nearly six prime ministers, and it's been ten years since Brexit. So, I think, for the American public, we see a lot of changes. What ultimately led to Starmer's downfall?

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: I think when Starmer got elected two years ago on this mandate of change, that was the Labour Party's slogan into the last election almost exactly two years ago, there was a real desire and a real hope that they would be different to the conservative party that had gone before, who had, as you say, had run through a whole host of different prime ministers.
paragraphs.cn: I think when Starmer was elected two years ago on a mandate for change -- it's almost two years since Labour's slogan in the last election -- people really wanted and hoped they would be different from the previous Conservative Party, which, as you said, had gone through a string of different prime ministers.

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: Keir Starmer's pitch was, I'm the man to bring stability and order to British politics, but they basically made a series of what felt like unforced errors right at the start, which really set the tone. They decided to cut winter fuel allowance for elderly people in a move that was kind of seen as really typically un- Labour. And also there was a scandal over Keir Starmer accepting free gifts as prime minister, like a suit, glasses, those kind of things.
paragraphs.cn: Keir Starmer's campaign slogan was, 'I am the one who can bring stability and order to British politics,' but they made a series of seemingly unforced errors at the start, which really set the tone. They decided to cut winter fuel payments for the elderly, a move seen as very un-Labour. Additionally, there was a scandal involving Keir Starmer accepting free gifts as prime minister, like suits, glasses, and such.

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: Kind of sounds like small things, but this really at the start of those two years, set the framing, set the tone for people to kind of start to wonder, actually, is this government very different to what we've seen before? Now, obviously, as it's developed, we've seen more recently there's been a real scandal over the appointment of Peter Mandelson, the ambassador to the United States.
paragraphs.cn: Sounds like small things, but it set the framework and tone in the first two years, making people wonder, is this government really that different from what we've seen before? Now, obviously, as things developed, we recently saw a real scandal over the appointment of Peter Mandelson as ambassador to the US.

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: And his judgment has really been called into question over that, as we found out more and more through the constant release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, that I know viewers on your side of the pond will be familiar with this story --
paragraphs.cn: With the ongoing release of Jeffrey Epstein documents, we learn more information, and his judgment has been questioned. I know your audience is familiar with this story --

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: As one of those that just seem to go away. And I think that is really what's thrown it more recently into a kind of state of crisis. So, yes, it's been a wild ride.
paragraphs.cn: Like something that never seems to go away. I think that's what recently pushed the situation into a crisis state. So, yes, it's been a crazy ride.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I want to -- as he was speaking this morning at Downing Street, I was like, is that music? There was like this music playing behind him. And it turned out it was protesters who were blasting the anthem for the E.U., because it has been almost ten years since the Brexit vote. But since Brexit, there's been the pandemic, the Ukrainian war, I didn't realize this, but an immigration surge as U.K. tried to mitigate some of the concerns of Brexit.
paragraphs.cn: I thought -- this morning when he was speaking on Downing Street, I thought, is that music? It sounded like music playing behind him. It turned out to be protesters playing the EU anthem, because it's almost ten years since the Brexit referendum. But since Brexit, there has also been the pandemic, the war in Ukraine, and I didn't expect the wave of immigration when the UK tried to ease some of the concerns from Brexit.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, does this put reform in a stronger position? Are we looking at another Labour leader? You know, people have been talking about Andy Burnham because he won -- this is the greater Manchester mayor. Is this still a battle of Labour versus reform? And if so, what does Starmer leaving say about that race?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，这是否让改革党处于更有利的地位？我们是否在关注另一位工党领袖？你知道，人们一直在谈论安迪·伯纳姆，因为他赢得了 -- -- 他是大曼彻斯特市长。这仍然是工党与改革党之间的较量吗？如果是这样，斯塔默的离开对这场竞争意味着什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: I think the first thing to say is, your point about Brexit is right. Essentially, what we've seen over the past decade is British voters trying to, in various ways, push a button marked change, whether that was for Brexit, whether that was for Boris Johnson, who was saying, I'm going to get Brexit done. Then the Tories had a whole host of different scandals. So, then they wanted change again with Keir Starmer. And they feel like they haven't got it.
paragraphs.cn: 我想首先要说的是，你关于脱欧的观点是对的。基本上，过去十年我们看到的是英国选民试图以各种方式按下"改变"按钮，无论是为了脱欧，还是为了鲍里斯·约翰逊 -- -- 他说"我要完成脱欧"。然后保守党出现了一系列不同的丑闻。所以，他们又希望通过基尔·斯塔默实现改变。但他们觉得并没有得到改变。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: So, now, two years in, Andy Burnham is presenting himself as the Labour person who can deliver that longed for change. But I think you're right. I think the real battle that is scaring a lot of Labour MPs is the threat of Nigel Farage and reform. And that's really what led to the most recent crisis was the reform party doing very well in the local elections, a.k.a. the mid-term elections here.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，现在两年过去了，安迪·伯纳姆将自己塑造成能够实现人们渴望的改变的工党人物。但我认为你是对的。我认为真正让许多工党议员感到害怕的威胁是奈杰尔·法拉奇和改革党。而这正是导致最近危机的原因 -- -- 改革党在地方选举（也就是这里的中期选举）中表现非常好。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: And so I think, unless Andy Burnham can -- who looks like he's going to take over for -- as prime minister, the very popular mayor of Manchester here, unless he can really deliver quickly in the next two to three years, there will be people again looking at reform going, well, we've tried everything now. We haven't got the change we wanted. We voted Brexit. That didn't work. We voted for a whole host of different prime ministers. Nothing is working.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，我认为，除非安迪·伯纳姆 -- -- 他看起来将接任首相，他是这里非常受欢迎的曼彻斯特市长 -- -- 除非他能在未来两三年内迅速取得成果，否则人们会再次看向改革党，心想：我们现在什么都试过了。我们没有得到想要的改变。我们投票支持脱欧，但没成功。我们投票选出了一系列不同的首相。什么都不管用。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.en: And so, reforms pitch is very much, we are the insurgent populist party that can actually smash up the status quo, smash up the establishment and finally deliver change. But it's kind of a -- a bit of a Houdini act, really, because, obviously, Nigel Farage was one of the main architects of Brexit in the first place and many feel that Brexit hasn't been a success.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，改革党的口号非常明确：我们是反叛的民粹主义政党，能够真正打破现状，打破建制，最终实现改变。但这有点像霍迪尼式的逃脱术，因为显然，奈杰尔·法拉奇本身就是脱欧的主要设计者之一，而许多人认为脱欧并不成功。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Well, Patrick Baker, thank you so much. You're going to have a busy day ahead. Appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好的，帕特里克·贝克，非常感谢。你今天会很忙。感谢你。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now I want to turn to Italy because the president has doubled down on his feud with the prime minister there. And it's basically over this photo. Trump posted on Truth Social that Giorgia Meloni is, quote, "doing poorly in Italy with her level of popularity." So Meloni responded saying, quote, "my popularity is none of your concern, and I suggest you focus on yours." Now, this back and forth basically started when the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 现在我想转向意大利，因为总统加大了他与意大利总理的争执。而这基本上源于这张照片。特朗普在Truth Social上发帖称，乔治娅·梅洛尼"在意大利的人气很低"。梅洛尼回应说，"我的人气不关你的事，我建议你关注自己的人气。"这场来回争执基本上始于美国

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: president accused the Italian prime minister of begging for a photo with him during the G7 Summit last week, which is not how she remembers it. GIORGIA MELONI, ITALIAN PRIME MINISTER (through translator): Donald Trump's statements are completely made up. I am frankly astonished. I don't know why the president of the United States behaves like this toward his allies. There is one thing he should remember, neither I nor Italy ever beg.
paragraphs.cn: 总统上周在七国集团峰会期间指责意大利总理乞求与他合影，但她的记忆并非如此。意大利总理乔治娅·梅洛尼（通过翻译）表示：特朗普的言论完全是捏造的。我对此感到震惊。我不知道为什么美国总统对他的盟友如此行事。他应该记住一件事，我和意大利从未乞求过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Group chat is back. With friends like these, as we say. I don't know who wants to go first, but why do you think this is persisting the way it is? ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": Well, look, Giorgia Meloni is somebody who is seen as a staunch ally of the president. She was one of the only -- I think the only foreign dignitary who attended his inauguration in 2025.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。就像我们说的，有这样的朋友。我不知道谁想先发言，但你认为为什么这件事会持续下去？《NOTUS》国家政治记者埃琳娜·施奈德：嗯，乔治娅·梅洛尼被视为总统的坚定盟友。她是唯一一位 -- -- 我想是唯一一位出席他2025年就职典礼的外国政要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This has been a really warm, strong relationship where she was sort of seen as the leader amongst NATO leaders who could really reach the president. And I think it is really notable that this spat has continued because I think it reflects his larger frustration with NATO allies over this U.S.-Iran conflict. And let's not forget that this spat sort of started a couple of weeks ago when he -- when Donald Trump attacked the pope and then Giorgia Meloni, you know, defended the pope in light of the U.S.-Iran War.
paragraphs.cn: 这段关系一直非常温暖、牢固，她被视为北约领导人中能够真正与总统沟通的领袖。我认为这场争执持续下去非常值得注意，因为这反映了他对北约盟友在美伊冲突问题上的更大不满。别忘了，这场争执大约几周前就开始了，当时唐纳德·特朗普攻击了教皇，然后乔治娅·梅洛尼在美伊战争背景下为教皇辩护。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There is -- let's go back to the offense. The offense is not backing up Trump when the pope stood up to him. JULIA MANCHESTER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "THE HILL": Absolutely. And we know that Giorgia Meloni has been, like you said. Elena, an ally of the president. She spoke at things like CPAC, for example. She's been a popular figure even within the American conservative movement.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们回到冒犯之处。冒犯之处在于，当教皇对抗特朗普时，她没有支持特朗普。《国会山报》国家政治记者朱莉娅·曼彻斯特：完全正确。我们知道乔治娅·梅洛尼一直像你说的那样，是总统的盟友。她曾在例如CPAC等活动上发言。她甚至在美国保守派运动中也是一个受欢迎的人物。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Because they've backed a lot of right-wing parties in Europe.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。因为他们支持了欧洲许多右翼政党。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's their whole thing.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是他们的全部。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: But, you know, taking a step back from that, it's fascinating to see sort of, you know, I guess, I don't want to say the decline, but maybe the tensions that are rising between Trump and many European leaders, like Meloni, at this time during NATO -- because of the Iraq war, NATO tensions, et cetera.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，退一步看，看到特朗普与许多欧洲领导人（如梅洛尼）之间在此时因伊拉克战争、北约紧张局势等而出现的紧张关系，甚至可以说是某种程度的恶化，这很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, humiliates Meloni.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以，羞辱了梅洛尼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Starmer steps down. He kicks dirt over it by pre-announcing it.
paragraphs.cn: 斯塔默辞职了。他提前宣布这一消息，试图掩盖此事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I don't really know who's left.
paragraphs.cn: 我真的不知道还有谁留下。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: Yes, but at the same time though, in terms of who's left, we see --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但与此同时，就留下的人而言，我们看到 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: Yes. The administration is turning their direction towards Latin America. And yesterday we did see the right wing, Trump-backed conservative leader in Colombia's election win.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。政府正将目光转向拉丁美洲。昨天我们确实看到，哥伦比亚选举中，特朗普支持的右翼保守派领导人获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: So, you're seeing, I think, more of this support from the Trump administration in Latin America as they maybe turn away from Europe.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为你会看到特朗普政府在拉丁美洲给予更多支持，因为他们可能正在远离欧洲。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, just in terms of this battle with Italy, the foreign minister actually just canceled a trip to the U.S. where they were going to have a business investment and science forum in Miami. Marco Rubio was supposed to be there. I mean, I don't know how big the business is with Italy, but they're trying to make a point here by saying, we're not showing up to this thing.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，就与意大利的这场争端而言，外交部长实际上刚刚取消了前往美国的行程，他们原计划在迈阿密举办一个商业投资和科学论坛。马可·鲁比奥本应出席。我的意思是，我不知道与意大利的生意有多大，但他们试图通过说"我们不会出席"来表明立场。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And when I looked at the papers this morning there, just a quote here from somebody, this is in, I think, "Libero." This person says, "I hate to say it, but I can't find, and perhaps there isn't, another way to say it: Donald Trump is an idiot. Period." ELEANOR MUELLER, WHITE HOUSE ECONOMY POLICY REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": It's worth reiterating. I mean these are people who agree with Trump ideologically. And we've seen similar splits with folks who, again, think similarly to him in France and Germany.
paragraphs.cn: 今天早上我看报纸时，看到有人引用了一句话，我想是来自《自由报》。这个人说："我不想这么说，但我找不到，也许根本没有别的说法：唐纳德·特朗普是个白痴。句号。"白宫经济政策记者埃莉诺·米勒（来自"Semafor"）表示：值得重申。我的意思是，这些人是在意识形态上同意特朗普的人。我们在法国和德国也看到了类似的分裂，那些人同样与特朗普想法相似。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's not just Carney in Canada who is not on the same page as Trump on many of these things to begin with. It's people who should be his strongest allies abroad. And because --
paragraphs.cn: 不仅仅是加拿大的卡尼在许多事情上与特朗普意见不一。这些人本应是他在国外最坚定的盟友。而且因为 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But she also has wiggle room. Like, when I look at the approval ratings between Trump and Meloni and the pope, like, she's got room -- if you're going to back somebody, are you going to back the guy with 78 percent approval rating or 36?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但她也有回旋余地。比如，当我看到特朗普、梅洛尼和教皇的支持率时，她是有空间的 -- -- 如果你要支持一个人，你会支持支持率78%的人还是36%的人？

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: It's a political calculation. They know that Trump is not only polling well in the United States, but he's not polling well in their countries. And so, if you've -- you're looking to pull yourself up a little bit, this is maybe an excuse to do it by leaning into these clashes that Trump seems so willing to start unprompted.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah perhitungan politik. Mereka tahu Trump tidak hanya populer di jajak pendapat AS, tetapi juga tidak populer di negara mereka. Jadi, jika Anda ingin sedikit meningkatkan diri, ini mungkin menjadi alasan, dengan terlibat dalam konflik yang tampaknya ingin dimulai Trump tanpa alasan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: One more thing. Is it also maybe because, like, they're not -- they didn't fare so well in the tariff conversations. Like, it's not clear to me what the benefit is to be a friend of Trump in this scenario.
paragraphs.cn: Satu hal lagi. Mungkinkah juga karena mereka tidak tampil baik dalam negosiasi tarif? Misalnya, saya tidak tahu apa keuntungan menjadi teman Trump dalam situasi ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Like slightly less tariffs or?
paragraphs.cn: Misalnya, sedikit mengurangi tarif?

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: I think that's a totally fair point. I mean, look, he has not only obviously gone after just Italy, but a whole host of European countries. Let's not forget where a lot of this also started, which was his attempt to take over Greenland, where he refused to rule out military force in an attempt to go after a NATO ally. So, look, this is -- this has been many years now in this second Trump term where we have seen him really go after allies in addition to who he sees as adversaries.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir itu masuk akal. Maksud saya, lihat, dia jelas tidak hanya menargetkan Italia, tetapi juga banyak negara Eropa. Jangan lupa, banyak hal juga dimulai dari sana, seperti usahanya mengambil alih Greenland, ketika dia menolak untuk mengecualikan penggunaan kekuatan terhadap sekutu NATO. Jadi, lihat, di masa jabatan kedua Trump, kita sudah melihat dia menargetkan sekutu sekaligus lawan yang dia anggap.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But oddly self-inflicted. OK, you guys, stay with me. It's now 45 minutes past the hour. This is our morning roundup. Republican Senator Lindsey Graham warning President Trump not to let a critical foreign surveillance tool lapse any longer. Trump stalled his DNI pick, Jay Clayton's confirmation after learning that Democrats were going to let the FISA rule laps, something that Graham argues is playing with fire. SEN.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tapi anehnya itu bumerang. Baiklah, semuanya, tetap ikuti kami. Sekarang pukul 45 menit lewat. Ini adalah ringkasan pagi kami. Senator Republik Lindsey Graham memperingatkan Presiden Trump agar tidak membiarkan alat pengawasan asing yang penting berakhir lagi. Trump menunda konfirmasi calon Direktur Intelijen Nasional Jay Clayton setelah mengetahui Demokrat akan membiarkan aturan Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act berakhir, dan Graham menganggap ini bermain api.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: LINDSEY GRAHAM (R-SC): And I would urge President Trump to let Clayton testify and that we nominate Clayton and get him affirmed as the new DNI. We don't want FISA to go dark. There's too many threats to our country for 702 to go down. Anybody who owns the shutting down of 702 under FISA will own a future attack against the United States.
paragraphs.cn: Senator Lindsey Graham (Republik, South Carolina): Saya mendesak Presiden Trump untuk meminta Clayton bersaksi, kami mencalonkan Clayton dan mengonfirmasinya sebagai Direktur Intelijen Nasional yang baru. Kami tidak ingin Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act berakhir. Jika Section 702 berakhir, itu terlalu mengancam negara kami. Siapa pun yang menyebabkan Section 702 di bawah Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act berakhir, akan bertanggung jawab atas serangan terhadap AS di masa depan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Graham also says that the top Democrat on the intelligence committee tells him there are enough Democratic votes to reauthorize FISA if Clayton's nomination is confirmed. And dozens of residents in Louisiana displaced this morning after flood waters from Tropical Storm Arthur. This is reportedly the most flooding the area has seen in decades.
paragraphs.cn: Graham juga mengatakan bahwa Demokrat tertinggi di komite intelijen memberitahunya bahwa jika pencalonan Clayton dikonfirmasi, akan ada cukup suara Demokrat untuk memperbarui Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act. Pagi ini, puluhan warga di Louisiana mengungsi akibat banjir yang disebabkan oleh Badai Tropis Arthur. Dilaporkan ini adalah banjir terparah di wilayah itu dalam beberapa dekade.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: We're blessed to not have any fatalities.
paragraphs.cn: Kami beruntung tidak ada korban jiwa.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: We just decided to come in today and see what we could do to help.
paragraphs.cn: Kami memutuskan untuk datang hari ini melihat apa yang bisa kami bantu.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: What are you going to do?
paragraphs.cn: Apa yang akan kamu lakukan?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: A flood warning for the area is in effect through tonight. And Serena Williams set to make another big return at Wimbledon. Williams accepted a wild card invitation to play singles. The 44-year- old recently returned to competition after announcing her retirement four years back. Williams had already planned to play at Wimbledon, competing in doubles with her sister Venus. And Wimbledon starts next week. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, is Gavin Newsom betting on Biden.
paragraphs.cn: Peringatan banjir di wilayah itu akan berlangsung hingga malam ini. Serena Williams bersiap untuk comeback lagi di Wimbledon. Williams menerima undangan wildcard untuk tunggal. Pemain berusia 44 tahun ini baru-baru ini kembali ke lapangan setelah mengumumkan pensiun empat tahun lalu. Williams awalnya berencana bermain ganda di Wimbledon, berpasangan dengan kakaknya Venus. Wimbledon dimulai minggu depan. Selanjutnya di CNN This Morning, Gavin Newsom bertaruh pada Biden.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The group chat will be back to discuss the new reporting on why he might be leaning in on the former president's legacy ahead of 2028.
paragraphs.cn: Group chat akan kembali membahas laporan baru tentang mengapa dia mungkin cenderung mewarisi warisan politik mantan presiden sebelum 2028.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, there's a new report in "Axios" providing some insight into a possible Gavin Newsom presidential run. Newsom is reportedly gambling on embracing former President Joe Biden, thinking that will be a popular position among Democrats. Even after Biden had to drop out of his re-election bid, Newsom has still spoken favorably of Biden's presidency. GOV. GAVIN NEWSOM (D-CA): His experiences mirror so many of my fathers. And I see my father in so many ways, the way I see President Biden.
paragraphs.cn: Situs Axios merilis laporan baru yang mengungkap beberapa insider tentang kemungkinan Gavin Newsom mencalonkan diri sebagai presiden. Dilaporkan, Newsom bertaruh dengan mendukung mantan Presiden Joe Biden, percaya ini akan populer di kalangan Demokrat. Bahkan setelah Biden harus mundur dari pencalonan kembali, Newsom tetap memuji masa kepresidenan Biden. Gubernur California Gavin Newsom: Pengalamannya sangat mirip dengan pengalaman ayah saya. Saya melihat ayah saya dalam banyak hal, sama seperti saya melihat Presiden Biden.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's one of the reasons I was so loyal to the end and I happily will take the arrows on that and defend that. And we'll continue to build on the legacy, I would argue, of our former president, who I think was one of the most successful presidents in the last century, and that is Joe Biden. And I will defend that to my grave.
paragraphs.cn: Itulah salah satu alasan saya setia sampai akhir, saya dengan senang hati menerima kritik dan membelanya. Kami akan terus melanjutkan warisan mantan presiden kami, yang saya anggap sebagai salah satu presiden paling sukses di abad lalu, yaitu Joe Biden. Saya akan membelanya sampai mati.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Group chat is back. So, I don't know how you split the Biden presidency and whatever it's legislative achievements were, or economic achievements were, with the decision to run again and the meltdown that happened in the process.
paragraphs.cn: Group chat kembali. Saya tidak tahu bagaimana memisahkan masa kepresidenan Biden dan pencapaian legislatif atau ekonominya, dari keputusannya untuk mencalonkan diri lagi dan kehancuran yang terjadi dalam prosesnya.

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: Very difficult calculus to do that I think a lot of Democratic hopefuls have decided is not possible. We've seen folks like Brian Schatz (ph) happily critique Biden for his performance across the (INAUDIBLE), not just at the end.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah trade-off yang sangat sulit, dan saya pikir banyak calon potensial Demokrat menganggapnya mustahil. Kita melihat orang-orang seperti Brian Schatz, yang tidak hanya pada akhirnya, tetapi sepanjang proses dengan senang hati mengkritik kinerja Biden.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, everyone coming out with their books and, yes, either --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, semua orang menulis buku, entah -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: Exactly. But I don't think anyone can accuse Gavin Newsom of not being a very calculating politician. And so, clearly, he thinks that there is benefit to be found here. Whether that's, you know, Biden's support with black and Latinx voters or what.
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Tapi saya pikir tidak ada yang bisa menuduh Gavin Newsom bukan politisi yang cermat. Jadi, jelas dia melihat ada keuntungan di sini. Entah itu dukungan Biden di kalangan pemilih kulit hitam dan Latin, atau apa pun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, let's show that. His favorability with black voters right now, 53 percent for Biden remaining and with Latino voters, 40 percent. We know that, especially after black and Latino men and some nominal but helpful numbers went to Trump, that they'd like to bring this community back. And also I want to show you one more set of numbers, favorability ratings for Trump versus Biden, because they're close. And if you're Trump, you don't want them close. But if you're Newsom, you're like, ah, a window.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, mari kita tunjukkan. Saat ini, tingkat kesukaan Biden di kalangan pemilih kulit hitam masih 53%, dan di kalangan pemilih Latin 40%. Kita tahu, terutama setelah pria kulit hitam dan Latin serta beberapa dukungan nominal tetapi berguna beralih ke Trump, mereka ingin merebut kembali kelompok ini. Saya juga ingin menunjukkan data lain, perbandingan tingkat kesukaan Trump vs Biden, karena keduanya dekat. Jika Anda Trump, Anda tidak ingin mereka dekat. Tapi jika Anda Newsom, Anda berpikir, ah, ada peluang.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: Not so bad. But, you know, it is certainly, I think, a gamble for Gavin Newsom to do this right now. He is calculating on the point about black and Latino voters. That's certainly a very good point, especially as President Trump loses support from that bloc, from when he gained support with, you know, a certain amount of support in 2024. But I think it comes at a time when you're seeing the Democratic Party start to maybe go in a different direction.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak terlalu buruk. Tapi Anda tahu, saya pikir Gavin Newsom melakukan ini sekarang memang sebuah pertaruhan. Dia memperhitungkan soal pemilih kulit hitam dan Latin. Itu tentu argumen yang bagus, terutama setelah Presiden Trump kehilangan dukungan kelompok itu, yang dia dapatkan sampai batas tertentu di 2024. Tapi saya pikir, sekarang saatnya Demokrat mungkin mulai beralih ke arah yang berbeda.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: You know, you're seeing the popping up of these Democratic socialist candidates in a number of different parts of the country. And, you know, a lot of it could be ideological. But I do also think a lot of it is generational. So, for someone like --
paragraphs.cn: Anda tahu, Anda akan melihat kandidat sosialis demokrat ini muncul di banyak tempat di seluruh negeri. Dan, banyak yang mungkin ideologis. Tapi saya juga pikir banyak yang masalah generasi. Jadi, untuk -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, he thinks that's the opening for him. Like you can grab all those people who are a little bit older --
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, dia pikir ini adalah kesempatannya. Misalnya, Anda bisa menarik mereka yang sedikit lebih tua -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Aren't so enamored with the DSA or have big questions and say, don't worry, there's still a home for you. People who actually did kind of like Biden's policies.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka yang tidak terlalu tergila-gila dengan sosialis demokrat atau memiliki banyak pertanyaan, lalu berkata, jangan khawatir, masih ada tempat untukmu di sini. Mereka yang sebenarnya agak suka kebijakan Biden.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: And I think that he's very aware, as a lot of those potential 2028 candidates are, that South Carolina is still going to be intimately involved in the early nomination process, a state that has -- that where black voters are an enormously important part of the Democratic primary electorate there.
paragraphs.cn: Dan saya pikir dia sangat sadar, seperti banyak calon potensial 2028, bahwa South Carolina masih akan terlibat erat dalam proses pencalonan awal, negara bagian di mana pemilih kulit hitam adalah bagian yang sangat penting dari pemilih primer Demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But an older and slightly more conservative black voters.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. But they are slightly older, slightly more conservative Black voters.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: Exactly. Exactly what I mean. And that's what rocket launched Joe Biden's own presidency in 2020. And I think that there is a -- an awareness that you can't just alienate those folks by saying, let's throw out Joe Biden altogether. I think he's trying to thread this needle of acknowledging the successes that Joe Biden had. But I also --
paragraphs.cn: Right. That's exactly what I mean. And that's what boosted Joe Biden's presidential campaign in 2020. I think people realize you can't alienate these people by just saying "let's completely dump Joe Biden." I think he's trying to walk a fine line between acknowledging Joe Biden's successes. But I also -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And the, reminds me of my dad is very, like, instead of everyone being like, oh, this doddering magoo (ph) is a problem. He's like, you know what, my father. Like this, yes, let's remember.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. This reminds me of my father, who is very much like, not everyone is like "oh, this old fool is a problem." He would say, you know, my father. Like that, yes, let's remember.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: Let's remember the positive feelings about it. And also, to your point about generational, a distinction of, my father, as opposed to, my peer or something like that.
paragraphs.cn: Let's remember the positive feelings about it. And, regarding generational differences, you mentioned my father, not my peers or something like that.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, here's Hunter Biden. I got to play this one other thing because this Gavin Newsom podcast really great with the bookings. Here's Hunter Biden.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, this is Hunter Biden. I have to play this because Gavin Newsom's podcast is really great in terms of guest invitations. This is Hunter Biden.

paragraphs.speaker: Hunter Bidne
paragraphs.en: OK, wait, one thing is just, you've heard dad say it a million times is, I'll come campaign for you or against you, whatever helps most. And, you know, he would say -- GOV. GAVIN NEWSOM (D-CA): And put his finger in my stomach.
paragraphs.cn: Okay, wait, there's one thing, you've heard Dad say it countless times: I'll campaign for you or against you, whichever is most helpful. And, you know, he would say -- -- California Governor Gavin Newsom: And then poke his finger into my stomach.

paragraphs.speaker: Biden
paragraphs.en: Yes. And he would say it and you'd be like, you know, it was -- it was silly because of course you'd want him to come out. In my case, it's for real.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. He would say that, and then you'd think, you know, it's a bit silly because of course you want him to come out. In my case, it's true.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, he's joking, but Biden's been on a tour, Junior Biden, trying to rehabilitate. But he has a very clear-eyed way of talking about it.
paragraphs.cn: Well, he's joking, but Biden has been on tour, little Biden, trying to salvage his image. But the way he talks about it is very sober.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: He does. And it's interesting to see him on X in particular. And I understand that social media is not always real life --
paragraphs.cn: Indeed. Especially seeing him on platform X, it's interesting. I know social media isn't always real life -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: But he's becoming a personality and responding to the trolls, to maybe conservatives who are pushing back. So, that's been fascinating to watch.
paragraphs.cn: But he's becoming a personality, responding to trolls, maybe those conservative counter-attackers. So, it looks fun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, it's Monday. We all have a bunch of reporting we're about to be assigned. And to get ahead of those editor assignments that we don't like, we have ideas of our own. What's in your group chat, Eleanor?
paragraphs.cn: Alright, it's Monday. We all have a lot of reporting tasks to assign. To avoid those editing tasks we don't like, we have our own ideas. Eleanor, what's in your group chat?

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: I don't think my editor is going to go over this one.
