paragraphs.cn: I don't think my editor would agree with this.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, no? No? It's all right.
paragraphs.cn: Oh, no? No? That's okay.

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.en: We adopted a dog recently. His name is Stanley. And we got him from Old Dominion Humane Society in Fredericksburg, Virginia. They are amazing. He's four years old. He was saved from a hoarder situation. Some lady had 70 dogs in her RV. So, again, shout out to Old Dominion Humane Society. And we are so happy to have Stanley.
paragraphs.cn: We recently adopted a dog. His name is Stanley. We adopted him from the Old Dominion Humane Society in Fredericksburg, Virginia. They are great. He is four years old and was rescued from a hoarder. A woman had 70 dogs in her RV. So, again, thank you Old Dominion Humane Society. We are happy to have Stanley.

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Manchester
paragraphs.en: My group chat is World Cup related.
paragraphs.cn: My group chat is about the World Cup.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: So, Sam Adams' parent company, The Boston Beer Company, said in a news release last year that basically the Tartan Army, which are the Scottish fans that are taking over the city of Boston, drank four times what the company normally stocks during a typical four day weekend, including the Fourth of July. So, once again, the Scots putting Bostonians to shame.
paragraphs.cn: So, Samuel Adams' parent company Boston Beer Company said in a press release last year that, basically, the Scottish fans occupying Boston, the "Tartan Army," drank four times the company's usual stock during a typical four-day weekend including Independence Day. So, the Scots embarrassed Bostonians again.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is breaking news in my hometown.
paragraphs.cn: This is breaking news from my hometown.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Like, the idea that you could like go to Boston, Fenway.
paragraphs.cn: Like, you can go to Fenway Park in Boston.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I mean, people know how to drink there, OK. And they just -- they embarrassed them.
paragraphs.cn: I mean, people there know how to drink, okay. They just -- -- they embarrassed those people.

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.en: They're putting them to shame. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: They made them feel inferior. Yes.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: They embarrassed them.
paragraphs.cn: They embarrassed them.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: Well, I have to say, mine is also World Cup related. The whole Elmo dust up has been really fun to watch because there's nothing I love more than like a low stakes drama in which like we're all sort of worried about Elmo's safety and security in this moment. But no, seriously, another sort of New York related thing is we've got elections, primaries in New York coming up tomorrow.
paragraphs.cn: Well, I have to say, my topic is also related to the World Cup. The whole Elmo drama looks fun, because nothing I like more than this low-stakes drama, and we're all worried about Elmo's safety right now. But seriously, another New York-related thing is that tomorrow New York will hold elections and primaries.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: And I think what's going to come out of those races, those primaries, is going to be so telling about where the Democratic Party is headed.
paragraphs.cn: I think the results of these races and primaries will be very telling of the Democratic Party's direction.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: And I think it's going to be a fascinating night that we're all going to be up late watching.
paragraphs.cn: I think it will be a fascinating night, and we'll all stay up late to watch.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And what is Zohran Mamdani's power beyond himself, right?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, apa pengaruh Zoran Mamdani selain dirinya sendiri?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's always the thing. You can have a big win, but do you have the ability to propel others around you? I think that's something people are going to be watching very closely.
paragraphs.cn: Hal ini selalu seperti itu. Anda bisa meraih kemenangan besar, tetapi apakah Anda mampu mendorong orang-orang di sekitar Anda? Saya pikir ini adalah hal yang akan dicermati orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.en: And he's sticking his neck out in three different house races. And it's going to be really fascinating to see whether or not that works.
paragraphs.cn: Dia mengambil risiko dalam tiga kampanye DPR yang berbeda. Akan sangat menarik untuk melihat apakah ini berhasil.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, you guys, thank you so much for being with us. I want to bring people up to speed on one more thing. We are awaiting remarks from the vice president, J.D. Vance. He is in Switzerland for the latest in those U.S. and Iran talks. Basically, in the last 24 hours, he's arrived there to launch the talks. And there's been a lot of friction. Last week we were reporting to you about continued exchange of fire between Hezbollah and Lebanon and Israel.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, teman-teman, terima kasih banyak atas kebersamaan Anda. Saya ingin memberi Anda satu pembaruan lagi. Kami sedang menunggu pidato Wakil Presiden J.D. Vance. Dia berada di Swiss untuk mengikuti putaran terbaru negosiasi AS-Iran. Pada dasarnya, dalam 24 jam terakhir, dia tiba di sana untuk memulai negosiasi. Namun, kedua belah pihak terus berselisih. Minggu lalu kami melaporkan baku tembak yang sedang berlangsung antara Hizbullah, Lebanon, dan Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This has become a major sticking point in these talks as Tehran tries to make it part of the overall ceasefire deal. And the Israeli government's frustrations boiling over with the U.S. because they weren't a part of this process at all. So, I think a lot of people are waiting to see how J.D. Vance will thread this needle. And this is day two of these negotiations. The technical teams are supposed to sit down.
paragraphs.cn: Ini telah menjadi kendala utama dalam negosiasi karena Teheran berusaha memasukkannya sebagai bagian dari kesepakatan gencatan senjata menyeluruh. Dan pemerintah Israel sangat frustrasi karena mereka sama sekali tidak terlibat dalam proses ini. Jadi, saya pikir banyak orang menunggu bagaimana J.D. Vance akan mengatasi masalah ini. Hari ini adalah hari kedua negosiasi. Tim teknis seharusnya duduk bersama.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: By technical we're talking about the details to be ironed out around enriched uranium and other issues around Iran's nuclear program, which are currently, based on this deal, at status quo. So, please stay with us. We are awaiting J.D. Vance to come to that podium. I'm Audie Cornish. And the headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Yang dimaksud dengan tingkat teknis adalah rincian yang perlu diselesaikan, termasuk pengayaan uranium dan masalah lain dari program nuklir Iran, yang menurut perjanjian saat ini, semuanya tetap pada status quo. Jadi, pantau terus kami. Kami sedang menunggu J.D. Vance naik ke podium. Saya Oti Cornish. Berikutnya adalah ringkasan berita.

vocabulary.en: an act of giving up a job or position
vocabulary.excerpt: Keir Starmer says he is resigning.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Keir Starmer mengatakan dia akan mengundurkan diri.

vocabulary.en: increasing or growing pressure
vocabulary.excerpt: The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour after mounting pressure to step aside.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Perdana Menteri Inggris mengumumkan pengunduran dirinya pada jam terakhir setelah menghadapi tekanan yang semakin meningkat untuk turun.

vocabulary.en: to resign from a position or allow someone else to take over
vocabulary.excerpt: mounting pressure to step aside.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Tekanan yang semakin meningkat untuk mundur.

vocabulary.en: the decisive or conclusive factor
vocabulary.excerpt: the clinching factor for this, Audie, after months of difficulties
vocabulary.exampleCn: Audie，经过数月的困难，决定性因素是……

vocabulary.en: elections for local government positions
vocabulary.excerpt: we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们在五月份英国各地举行了地方选举。

vocabulary.en: to increase suddenly and strongly
vocabulary.excerpt: the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 由奈杰尔·法拉奇领导的右翼民粹主义改革党支持率激增。

vocabulary.en: an event that warns someone to take action
vocabulary.excerpt: That was a real wake-up call.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 那是一个真正的警钟。

vocabulary.en: on the other hand; conversely
vocabulary.excerpt: But on the flip side, we also have this issue where Andy Burnham...
vocabulary.exampleCn: 但另一方面，我们还有安迪·伯纳姆的问题……

vocabulary.en: an election held to fill a vacancy that occurs between regular elections
vocabulary.excerpt: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week
vocabulary.exampleCn: 上周在英格兰西北部举行了一场特别选举。

vocabulary.en: to come together to form one group or mass
vocabulary.excerpt: it all coalesced around this point.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 所有因素都围绕这一点汇聚。

vocabulary.en: a smooth and organized transfer of power or responsibility
vocabulary.excerpt: I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我将尽一切努力确保权力的有序交接。

vocabulary.en: clear and unambiguous support
vocabulary.excerpt: I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我还将给予我的继任者充分且明确的支持。

vocabulary.en: the third attempt is likely to succeed
vocabulary.excerpt: Burnham, this will be basically third time's the charm
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伯纳姆，这基本上会是第三次好运。

vocabulary.en: to attack or criticize aggressively
vocabulary.excerpt: Farage has come out swinging today
vocabulary.exampleCn: Farage hari ini melancarkan serangan keras.

vocabulary.en: the authority to act granted by voters
vocabulary.excerpt: Andy Burnham doesn't ... have a mandate to lead.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Andy Burnham tidak memiliki mandat untuk memimpin.

vocabulary.en: the deliberate destruction of property
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump blames vandals for his Reflecting Pool problems.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Presiden Trump menyalahkan perusak atas masalah kolam refleksi.

vocabulary.en: an official summons or ticket for a minor offense
vocabulary.excerpt: they issued citations to five others.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Mereka mengeluarkan surat panggilan kepada lima orang lainnya.

vocabulary.en: a project to restore something to good condition
vocabulary.excerpt: his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed
vocabulary.exampleCn: Proyek perbaikannya yang menghabiskan biaya $14 juta gagal.

vocabulary.en: full of (something unpleasant)
vocabulary.excerpt: the water riddled with algae
vocabulary.exampleCn: Air penuh dengan ganggang.

vocabulary.en: simple plants that grow in water
vocabulary.excerpt: the water riddled with algae
vocabulary.exampleCn: Air penuh dengan ganggang.

vocabulary.en: to pursue legal action to the maximum degree allowed
vocabulary.excerpt: These are cases that will be prosecuted to the full extent.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kasus-kasus ini akan dihukum berat sesuai hukum.

vocabulary.en: a visually striking performance or display
vocabulary.excerpt: the World Cup now a must-see spectacle both on and off the field.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Piala Dunia kini menjadi tontonan wajib di dalam dan luar lapangan.

vocabulary.en: contrary to what common sense would suggest
vocabulary.excerpt: Is that smart or a counterintuitive strategy?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apakah ini langkah bijak atau strategi yang kontra-intuitif?

vocabulary.en: to behave confidently and make others recognize their importance
vocabulary.excerpt: it is a moment for reform to try to assert themselves.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ini adalah saat partai reformasi mencoba menegaskan kekuatannya.

sentences.en: The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour after mounting pressure to step aside.
sentences.cn: Perdana Menteri Inggris mengumumkan pengunduran dirinya dalam satu jam terakhir setelah tekanan yang semakin besar untuk mundur.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour; Keterangan waktu: after mounting pressure to step aside.
sentences.analysis: Frasa partisip sekarang sebagai predikat, menunjukkan waktu sekarang; 'mounting pressure' adalah ekspresi umum dalam berita, berarti 'tekanan yang semakin besar'; 'step aside' adalah eufemisme untuk 'mengundurkan diri'.

sentences.en: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election.
sentences.cn: Pertanyaan yang diajukan partai saya sekarang adalah apakah saya adalah orang yang paling tepat untuk memimpin kita ke pemilihan umum berikutnya.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: The question...is...; Klausa relatif: my party is asking now; Klausa pelengkap subjek: whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election.
sentences.analysis: 'best placed' adalah kolokasi tetap, berarti 'dalam posisi terbaik'; 'lead into' berarti 'memimpin menuju'.

sentences.en: Every decision I've taken has been about putting the country I love first.
sentences.cn: Setiap keputusan yang saya ambil adalah untuk mengutamakan negara yang saya cintai.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: Every decision...has been about...; Klausa relatif: I've taken; Objek: putting the country I love first.
sentences.analysis: 'putting...first' adalah frasa tetap, berarti 'mengutamakan...'; 'I love' adalah klausa relatif tanpa kata ganti relatif yang menerangkan 'the country'.

sentences.en: That is why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
sentences.cn: Itulah mengapa saya akan mengundurkan diri sebagai pemimpin Partai Buruh.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: That is; Klausa pelengkap subjek: why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
sentences.analysis: 'That is why...' adalah pola kalimat umum, berarti 'itulah mengapa...'; 'resign as' berarti 'mengundurkan diri dari jabatan...'.

sentences.en: The real clinching factor was that we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May, and the Labour Party suffered a massive defeat, losing more than 1,400 local council seats, even as we saw the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge.
sentences.cn: Faktor penentu yang sebenarnya adalah bahwa pada bulan Mei kami mengadakan pemilihan lokal di seluruh Inggris, dan Partai Buruh menderita kekalahan besar, kehilangan lebih dari 1.400 kursi dewan lokal, bahkan ketika kami melihat kebangkitan partai reformasi populis sayap kanan yang dipimpin oleh Nigel Farage.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: The real clinching factor was that...; Klausa pelengkap subjek: we had local elections...and the Labour Party suffered...; Keterangan penyerta: losing more than 1,400 local council seats; Klausa keterangan waktu: even as we saw...surge; Sisipan: led by Nigel Farage.
sentences.analysis: 'clinching factor' adalah istilah jurnalistik, berarti 'faktor penentu'; 'massive defeat' berarti 'kekalahan besar'; 'surge' sebagai kata kerja berarti 'melonjak, bangkit'; 'led by' adalah frasa partisip lampau sebagai penerang.

sentences.en: That seemed to suggest to certain parts of the Labour Party that they were going to struggle to defeat Reform in any general election.
sentences.cn: Hal itu tampaknya menunjukkan kepada sebagian kalangan di Partai Buruh bahwa mereka akan kesulitan mengalahkan partai reformasi dalam pemilihan umum mana pun.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama: That seemed to suggest...; Objek tidak langsung: to certain parts of the Labour Party; Klausa objek: that they were going to struggle to defeat Reform in any general election.
sentences.analysis: 'struggle to do' berarti 'berusaha keras untuk melakukan', di sini berarti 'sulit untuk'; 'defeat' berarti 'mengalahkan'.

sentences.en: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week, which he won so convincingly that it really strengthened the argument that he might be the man to defeat this right-wing populist party, Reform, in any upcoming election, since he had done it so decisively in this one constituency.
sentences.cn: 上周在英格兰西北部举行了一场特别选举，他以令人信服的方式获胜，这确实强化了一种论点，即他可能是任何即将到来的选举中击败这个右翼民粹主义政党改革党的人，因为他在这个选区如此果断地做到了这一点。
sentences.structure: 主句：There was a special election；定语从句：that was held...；非限制性定语从句：which he won...；结果状语从句：so...that...；同位语从句：the argument that...；原因状语从句：since he had done it...。
sentences.analysis: 'convincingly' 是副词，意为'令人信服地'；'strengthen the argument' 是'强化论点'；'decisively' 是'果断地'；'constituency' 是'选区'。

sentences.en: So, then, we sort of -- it all coalesced around this point.
sentences.cn: 所以，然后，我们有点 -- -- 这一切都围绕这一点凝聚起来。
sentences.structure: 主句：it all coalesced around this point；插入语：we sort of。
sentences.analysis: 'sort of' 是口语化表达，意为'有点、可以说'；'coalesce around' 是固定短语，意为'围绕...凝聚'。

sentences.en: Keir Starmer spent the weekend thinking about it, addressing the sort of political realities as they stood, and made this extremely difficult decision for him to step down.
sentences.cn: 基尔·斯塔默花了整个周末思考这件事，处理当时存在的各种政治现实，并做出了这个对他来说极其艰难的下台决定。
sentences.structure: 主句：Keir Starmer spent...thinking...addressing...and made...；时间状语：as they stood。
sentences.analysis: 'spend time doing' 是固定搭配；'address' 在这里意为'处理、应对'；'as they stood' 是固定短语，意为'按照当时的情况'；'step down' 是'辞职'。

sentences.en: I will remain in post as prime minister until the contest is complete, and I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power.
sentences.cn: 我将继续担任首相职务，直到竞选结束，我将尽我所能确保权力的有序交接。
sentences.structure: 并列句：I will remain...until...and I will do...；定语从句：I can；目的状语：to ensure an orderly handover of power。
sentences.analysis: 'remain in post' 是'留任'；'contest' 这里指'领导人竞选'；'handover of power' 是'权力交接'。

sentences.en: I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support, knowing that they will inherit a Britain that is far stronger and fairer than the one I inherited two years ago; better prepared for the challenges ahead; and better able to ensure the Labour Party secures a second term in office.
sentences.cn: 我还将给予我的继任者充分且明确的支持，因为我知道他们将继承一个比我两年前继承的更强大、更公平的英国；更好地为未来的挑战做好准备；并且更有能力确保工党赢得第二个任期。
sentences.structure: 主句：I will also give...；原因状语：knowing that...；宾语从句：they will inherit a Britain...；定语从句：that is far stronger...；比较结构：than the one I inherited...；并列结构：better prepared...and better able...。
sentences.analysis: 'unequivocal' 是'明确的'；'inherit' 是'继承'；'secure a second term' 是'赢得第二个任期'。

sentences.en: If they do, it could take a bit longer.
sentences.cn: 如果他们这样做，可能会花费更长的时间。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句：If they do；主句：it could take a bit longer。
sentences.analysis: 'do' 指代前文的'stand against Andy Burnham'；'a bit' 是程度副词，意为'一点'。

sentences.en: And pointing back to those local elections at the beginning of May, where Reform did so well.
sentences.cn: 并回溯到五月初的那些地方选举，改革党在那里表现非常好。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语：pointing back to...；定语从句：where Reform did so well。
sentences.analysis: 'point back to' 是'回溯'；'do well' 是'表现好'。

sentences.en: There are questions about whether they've peaked after Burnham's defeat of the reform candidate in that special election.
sentences.cn: 关于他们是否在伯纳姆在那次特别选举中击败改革党候选人后已经达到顶峰，存在疑问。
sentences.structure: 主句：There are questions about...；宾语从句：whether they've peaked after...。
sentences.analysis: 'peak' 作动词，意为'达到顶峰'；'defeat of' 是'击败'。

sentences.en: And the chance now for Labour under a new leader is to try to stabilize their grip on power so they can see off Reform in a general election when it happens.
sentences.cn: 现在工党在新领导人的领导下的机会是试图稳定他们对权力的掌控，以便他们能在即将到来的大选中击败改革党。
sentences.structure: 主句：the chance...is to try to...；目的状语：so they can see off...；时间状语从句：when it happens。
sentences.analysis: 'stabilize their grip on power' 是'稳定权力掌控'；'see off' 是固定短语，意为'击败、送走'。

sentences.en: Vandalizing or attempting to vandalize the Reflecting Pool will face the criminal justice system in D.C.
sentences.cn: 破坏或试图破坏倒影池的人将面临华盛顿特区的刑事司法系统。
sentences.structure: 主句：Vandalizing or attempting to vandalize...will face...。
sentences.analysis: 动名词短语作主语；'face' 意为'面临'；'criminal justice system' 是'刑事司法系统'。

sentences.en: The president now claiming that multiple people have been arrested, accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
sentences.cn: 总统现在声称多人被捕，被指控破坏林肯纪念堂倒影池。
sentences.structure: 主句：The president now claiming...；宾语从句：that multiple people have been arrested；过去分词短语：accused of...。
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作谓语；'accused of' 是'被指控'。

sentences.en: Now, this comes after his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed: the water riddled with algae, the lining ripped.
sentences.cn: 现在，这发生在他耗资1400万美元的修复项目失败之后：水中布满藻类，衬里撕裂。
sentences.structure: 主句：this comes after...；时间状语从句：his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed；冒号后为并列名词短语作补充说明。
sentences.analysis: 'comes after' 是'发生在...之后'；'rehabilitation project' 是'修复项目'；'riddled with' 是'充满'；'lining' 是'衬里'。

sentences.en: Visitors say they noticed an increased law enforcement presence following the president's post.
sentences.cn: 游客们说，他们注意到在总统发帖后，执法力量有所增加。
sentences.structure: 主句：Visitors say；宾语从句：they noticed an increased law enforcement presence；时间状语：following the president's post。
sentences.analysis: 'law enforcement presence' 是'执法力量存在'；'following' 是介词，意为'在...之后'。

sentences.en: They came up to me and two other women, asking if -- if we touched it and then just promoting us not to, that we would get arrested or charged with vandalism if we did.
sentences.cn: 他们走到我和另外两名女性面前，问我们是否碰过它，然后只是劝我们不要碰，说如果我们碰了就会被逮捕或被指控破坏公物。
sentences.structure: 主句：They came up to me and two other women；伴随状语：asking...and then promoting...；宾语从句：if we touched it；直接引语：that we would get arrested...if we did。
sentences.analysis: 'come up to' 是'走近'；'promote' 这里意为'劝告'；'charge with' 是'指控'。

topics.title: 英国首相辞职与工党领导权更迭
topics.content: 英国首相基尔·斯塔默（Keir Starmer）因工党在地方选举中失去1400多个席位、右翼改革党（Reform Party）崛起而被迫辞职。曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆（Andy Burnham）在特别选举中获胜，成为继任热门。斯塔默将留任至新领袖选出，强调有序交接。改革党领袖法拉奇（Nigel Farage）呼吁提前大选，称伯纳姆缺乏授权。
topics.keywords: resignation · local elections · Reform Party · special election · mandate

topics.title: 特朗普指控倒影池遭破坏与修复争议
topics.content: 特朗普声称林肯纪念堂倒影池（Reflecting Pool）遭人故意破坏，称已逮捕多人，但未提供证据。此前耗资1400万美元的修复工程失败，池水藻类泛滥、衬垫破裂。华盛顿特区检察官皮罗（Jeanine Pirro）表示将严惩破坏者。批评者认为问题源于工程失败，而非人为破坏。
topics.keywords: vandalism · Reflecting Pool · rehabilitation project · algae · citation

topics.title: 世界杯小国爆冷与文化融合
topics.content: 2026年世界杯上，库拉索（Curacao）等小国球队表现出色，成为赛事亮点。评论指出，赛前批评者低估了这些球队的实力，而赛场内外文化融合的场面也备受关注。
topics.keywords: World Cup · Curacao · spectacle · cultural embrace

topics.title: 美国政治动态：纽森与拜登家族互动
topics.content: 加州州长纽森（Gavin Newsom）在考虑竞选总统之际，与拜登家族互动频繁。亨特·拜登（Hunter Biden）表示愿意为纽森助选或反对其对手。分析认为这一策略可能反直觉，但有助于纽森争取民主党支持。
topics.keywords: Gavin Newsom · Hunter Biden · counterintuitive strategy · presidential campaign

quiz.question: 在 'mounting pressure to step aside' 中，'mounting' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: mounting 在此处意为'日益增加的'，与 increasing 同义。其他选项为干扰项。

quiz.question: 短语 'come out swinging' 在政治语境中通常表示？
quiz.explanation: come out swinging 是习语，意为'猛烈抨击'，常用于描述政客的激烈言论。

quiz.question: 下列哪个词最能描述 'the water riddled with algae' 中 'riddled with' 的含义？
quiz.explanation: riddled with 意为'充满（问题或不良物质）'，此处指水中满是藻类。

quiz.question: Menurut laporan, apa penyebab langsung pengunduran diri Keir Starmer?
options: B. Kekalahan telak Partai Buruh dalam pemilihan lokal
C. Konflik dengan Presiden AS Donald Trump
quiz.explanation: Laporan dengan jelas menyatakan bahwa Partai Buruh kehilangan lebih dari 1400 kursi dalam pemilihan lokal bulan Mei, dan kebangkitan Partai Reformasi menyebabkan tekanan internal yang meminta Starmer mundur.

quiz.question: Mengenai masalah kolam refleksi, apa pernyataan utama pemerintahan Trump?
options: A. Proyek perbaikan berhasil, tetapi dirusak
C. Kekurangan dana menyebabkan proyek terhenti
D. Manajemen buruk oleh National Park Service
quiz.explanation: Trump mengklaim kolam refleksi sengaja dirusak dan mengatakan banyak orang telah ditangkap, tetapi tidak memberikan bukti. Laporan juga menyebutkan kegagalan perbaikan, tetapi pemerintah menekankan tindakan perusakan.

quiz.question: Mengapa Andy Burnham dianggap sebagai kandidat kuat untuk pemimpin baru Partai Buruh?
options: A. Dia adalah perdana menteri saat ini
B. Dia memenangkan kemenangan telak dalam pemilihan khusus
C. Dia mendapat dukungan dari Partai Reformasi
D. Dia memiliki pengalaman kabinet yang luas
quiz.explanation: Laporan menunjukkan bahwa Burnham memenangkan kemenangan telak dalam pemilihan khusus di Inggris Barat Laut, membuktikan ia bisa mengalahkan kandidat Partai Reformasi, sehingga menjadi kandidat kuat.
